channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Anduril Raises $5 Billion in Funding and Is Valued at $61 Billion
item.description: The start-up, which makes A.I.-backed weapons, was valued at $61 billion in the financing round, double what it was a year ago.
item.dc:creator: Sheera Frenkel
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Kristian Thacker for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Fury, Anduril’s first autonomous air vehicle, at the company’s Arsenal 1 factory outside Columbus, Ohio.

item.title: Affordable N.Y.C. Homes Stay Empty for Months. That May Soon Change.
item.description: With affordable housing in extremely high demand, Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s administration is announcing plans to reduce red tape and get people into those apartments faster.
item.dc:creator: Mihir Zaveri
item.category: Department of Housing Preservation and Development (NYC)

media:content.media:description: Affordable apartments are extremely sought after in New York City, drawing millions of applications each year.

item.title: A Bronx Neighborhood Loses Its ‘Monarch’ to Arson
item.description: The police charged a 45-year-old man with three counts of homicide and one count of arson in a bodega fire that spread upstairs and killed a beloved resident.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Maslin Nir and Wesley Parnell

item.title: Picture Pormpt:
item.description: What are your favorite local budget-friendly places to eat?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Mamdani’s Opponents Raise More Than $1 Million to Fight His Agenda
item.description: The new group, which will be led by Jim Walden, a lawyer who ran for mayor last year, will run attack ads and be prepared to sue the Mamdani administration.
item.dc:creator: Sally Goldenberg

media:content.media:description: Jim Walden, who ran as an independent in last year’s mayoral election, accused Mayor Zohran Mamdani of policies that would endanger New York City.

item.title: Harvey Weinstein Faces Another Jury
item.description: The Manhattan jury is the third one to consider whether Mr. Weinstein, the disgraced Hollywood producer, raped an aspiring actress more than a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: Hurubie Meko and Kate Christobek

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Timothy A. Clary
media:content.media:description: A jury has begun deliberating in Harvey Weinstein’s rape case in Manhattan. It’s the third time the disgraced Hollywood producer has faced this charge.

item.title: ‘Spot’ Illustrations Prove There Are Many Ways to Draw One Thing
item.description: An artist explains her approach to an atypical assignment in the newspaper.
item.dc:creator: Times Insider Staff

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 13, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Jordan Klepper Wants the President to Get More Sleep
item.description: “Well, well, well, looks like the Sleepy Joe-er has become the sleepy Joe-ee,” Klepper said after President Trump was seen with his eyes closed during an event in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Qué significa implicit
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 245 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: implicit
item.description: This word has appeared in 245 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: México y EE. UU. rechazan que la CIA tuviera un papel letal en un operativo en México
item.description: Los desmentidos se produjeron después de un reportaje de CNN que afirmó que la CIA participó en el asesinato del integrante de un cártel a principios de año.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Maria Abi-Habib

media:content.media:credit: Carl De Souza/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: “El Gobierno de México rechaza categóricamente cualquier versión que pretenda normalizar, justificar o sugerir la existencia de operaciones letales, encubiertas o unilaterales de agencias extranjeras en territorio nacional”, dijo Omar García Harfuch, secretario de Seguridad y Protección Ciudadana de México.

item.title: Un informe israelí examina la violencia sexual durante y tras el atentado de Hamás
item.description: La investigación describe casos en los que se abusaba de las víctimas delante de sus familiares, o en los que los autores difundían imágenes de una víctima en redes sociales.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity

media:content.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Una casa en el kibutz Kfar Aza, Israel, que fue atacado el 7 de octubre de 2023.

item.title: Xi Jinping Is Planning for China’s Final Victory Over the U.S.
item.description: While Trump chases quick wins and flashy optics, Xi plays a generational game for global dominance.
item.dc:creator: Julian Gewirtz

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by The New York Times

item.title: El Reino Unido ha tenido 5 líderes en 10 años. ¿Está a punto de tener otro?
item.description: Los críticos del actual primer ministro, Keir Starmer piden su renuncia para que el Partido Laborista tenga una posibilidad en las elecciones generales antes de 2029.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia

media:content.media:description: Boris Johnson, left, Liz Truss and David Cameron

item.title: Europa prueba una nueva táctica para tratar a Trump: sin pedir disculpas
item.description: Los líderes europeos, atrapados en las secuelas de la guerra de Estados Unidos en Irán, han criticado al presidente públicamente. Cuando él se ha enfadado, ellos no han dado marcha atrás.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: El canciller alemán Friedrich Merz es uno de varios líderes europeos que han criticado abiertamente al presidente Trump.

item.title: Britain Has Had 5 Leaders in a Decade. Is It About to Get Another?
item.description: The promise of Keir Starmer’s Labour Party in 2024 was renewed stability — but that now appears to be under threat.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia

media:content.media:description: Boris Johnson, left, Liz Truss and David Cameron

item.title: Catherine, Princess of Wales, to Visit Italy
item.description: Catherine will visit Reggio Emilia, an Italian city celebrated for its approach to early childhood education.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo and Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:description: Catherine, Princess of Wales, in London last month.

item.title: Europe Tries a Trumpian Tactic With Trump: No Apologies
item.description: Stuck with the fallout from America’s war in Iran, European leaders have criticized the president publicly. When he’s been angered, they haven’t backed down.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz of Germany is one of several European leaders who has openly criticized President Trump.

item.title: Trump’s Shrinking Ambitions on China
item.description: The president came into office planning harsher trade moves on China than on the rest of the world. Here’s why he’s had to scale them back.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson

media:content.media:description: When President Trump tried to force China into making concessions last year by threatening extreme tariffs, the tactic backfired, forcing the United States to pare back its goals.

item.title: Corrections: May 13, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Wednesday, May 13, 2026.

item.title: How China Could Wield Its Control of Rare Earths Against Trump
item.description: A central question hanging over the summit this week is whether China will agree to extend a temporary postponement of even tougher rare-earth export controls.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher

media:content.media:description: Steel mills, chemical factories and rare-earth refineries in Baotou, China. The Trump administration said it was in frequent communication with China about rare earths.

item.title: Will Trump and Xi Try to Slow the A.I. Arms Race?
item.description: The leaders of both countries are expected to discuss the risks from artificial intelligence, but neither country is willing to be the first to slow down.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang

media:content.media:description: President Trump and President Xi Jinping meeting in Busan, South Korea, in 2025.

item.title: Here’s How Trump’s Trade War on China Ended Up in a Stalemate
item.description: As President Trump prepares to meet with Xi Jinping, a trade war that once threatened to freeze commerce between the two countries has given way to an uneasy truce.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson

media:content.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump imposed tariffs on Chinese goods far steeper than those on any other country.

item.title: The Lecturer and Philosopher King: Xi Jinping Behind Closed Doors
item.description: Encounters with other world leaders reveal a side of China’s leader that the public rarely sees, and offer clues to how he will approach President Trump in Beijing.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson

media:content.media:description: Xi Jinping, China’s leader, at the National People’s Congress in Beijing in March.

item.title: Quote of the Day: ‘He Got Me Every Time’: Some Pitchers Haunt Batters
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, May 13, 2026.

item.title: Nvidia C.E.O. Jensen Huang Hitches Ride With Trump to China After Last-Minute Invite
item.description: Jensen Huang boarded Air Force One in Alaska, joining a delegation of more than a dozen business leaders accompanying President Trump on his trip to Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Tripp Mickle and Ana Swanson

media:content.media:credit: Manuel Orbegozo for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jensen Huang, the chief executive of the Silicon Valley chip giant Nvidia, in San Jose, Calif., in March.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers: May 13, 2026
item.description: Brian Keller’s Wednesday puzzle changes our perspective.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Aronow

media:content.media:credit: Rozette Rago for The New York Times

item.title: U.S. and Mexican Officials Deny C.I.A. Had Lethal Role in Mexico Operation
item.description: The denials came after a CNN report claimed the C.I.A. had been involved in the assassination of a cartel member inside Mexico earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Maria Abi-Habib

media:content.media:credit: Carl De Souza/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: “The Government of Mexico categorically rejects any version that seeks to normalize, justify, or suggest the existence of lethal, covert, or unilateral operations by foreign agencies on national territory,” said Omar García Harfuch, Mexico’s security secretary

item.title: As Becerra Rises in Polls, He Draws Fire for Trying to Direct Interview
item.description: “This is not a ‘gotcha’ piece, right?” the leading Democratic candidate for California governor asked a television reporter before an interview.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín

media:content.media:description: Xavier Becerra, the health secretary in the Biden administration, is now the leading Democratic candidate in polls on the California governor’s race.

item.title: Dominican Republic Agrees to Take Third-Country Migrants Deported by U.S.
item.description: The agreement, which reverses President Luis Abinader’s previous stance, is part of President Trump’s efforts to find governments willing to accept deportees.
item.dc:creator: James Wagner and Hogla Enecia Pérez

media:content.media:description: President Luis Abinader of the Dominican Republic previously said, “We do not have, nor will we accept, nor are we obligated to, nor will we accept receiving people from other countries.”

item.title: Creamy Cabbage Pastina
item.description: This creamy cabbage pastina is a take on the simple Italian dish made from tiny pasta stars, chicken broth, egg yolks and Parmesan. It’s fortifying, comforting and tastes like a hug.

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Liebe for The New York Times

item.title: Cindy Burbank Wins Senate Primary in Nebraska, but Plans to Drop Out to Help Dan Osborn
item.description: Democrats in Nebraska appear headed toward the Senate matchup they want, while their primary for a key House district was too close to call.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning

media:content.media:credit: Walker Pickering for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Cindy Burbank, pictured during a recent motorcycle ride, won the Democratic nomination for the U.S. Senate in Nebraska, but she said she planned to drop out of the race in an effort to help Dan Osborn, an independent, win instead.

item.title: Nebraska Gov. Jim Pillen to Face Democrat Lynne Walz
item.description: Democrats face an uphill fight as they seek to unseat Gov. Jim Pillen, who is running for another term in the solidly Republican state.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith

media:content.media:description: Lynne Walz, a Democrat and former state legislator, is running for governor of Nebraska.

item.title: Trump’s ‘Golden Dome’ Missile Defense Plan Could Cost $1.2 Trillion
item.description: A report from the Congressional Budget Office said that space-based interceptors, which do not currently exist, would probably consume 60 percent of the total cost.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: President Trump during an announcement on his plan to develop the “Golden Dome” missile defense program at the White House last May.

item.title: Justice Dept. Officials Consider Settling Trump Suit Against I.R.S.
item.description: One of the settlement terms under review is for the I.R.S. to drop any audits of the president, his family members and businesses.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Duehren and Alan Feuer

media:content.media:description: President Trump sued the Internal Revenue Service in January over the leak of his tax returns during his first term.

item.title: Battle Over the Strait of Hormuz Leaves Safe Passage a Gamble
item.description: Vessels are traveling without transmitting critical information meant to keep the waters safe, maritime intelligence experts say.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Ships in the Strait of Hormuz near Oman on Friday.

item.title: In San Francisco, the Tents of Homeless People Are Disappearing
item.description: Mayor Daniel Lurie and city leaders have enforced bans on camping and moved people off sidewalks to reduce visible homelessness. Critics say too many homeless people are housed in jail.
item.dc:creator: Heather Knight

media:content.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Daniel Lurie of San Francisco spoke with a city worker in 2025 about cleaning up the Tenderloin neighborhood.

item.title: Deberíamos tomarnos más en serio el hantavirus
item.description: No sabemos cuándo llegará una nueva pandemia, pero deberíamos ser capaces de estar más preparados.
item.dc:creator: Zeynep Tufekci

item.title: Britain’s Post-Liberal Disorder
item.description: Britain’s post-liberal disorder.
item.dc:creator: Ross Douthat

media:content.media:credit: Photo illustration by The New York Times; source images by Christopher Furlong, Danny Lawson PA Images, John Keeble, and Matt Cardy, via Getty Images

item.title: Two Charged With Trafficking 260 Pounds of Meth to New Jersey
item.description: They face at least 10 years in prison, and the authorities accused one of the men of being associated with the Sinaloa Cartel.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk

media:content.media:description: Robert Frazer, U.S. attorney for the District of New Jersey. He announced the methamphetamine bust on Tuesday.

item.title: La historia de Kevin González, joven con cáncer que logró despedirse de sus padres deportados
item.description: Desde su cama de hospital en Chicago, el joven deseaba ver por última vez a sus padres. Pero tras cruzar ilegalmente desde México para visitarlo, fueron detenidos por inmigración en Arizona.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy

item.title: Anthropic in Talks to Raise Funding at a $950 Billion Valuation
item.description: The start-up, which recently released a powerful A.I. model called Mythos and is separately battling with the Pentagon, was previously valued at $380 billion.
item.dc:creator: Mike Isaac

media:content.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Anthropic’s developer conference in San Francisco this month.

item.title: Waymo Is Fixing All of Its Self-Driving Taxis to Avoid Floods
item.description: The company will recall its software after one of its robotaxis entered a flooded road during a heavy downpour in San Antonio last month.
item.dc:creator: Livia Albeck-Ripka

media:content.media:credit: Ariana Gomez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Waymo is recalling about 3,800 robotaxis to fix software that could allow the vehicle to “drive into standing water,” according to a letter on the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration’s website.

item.title: Kash Patel Denies Lying and Excessive Drinking in Heated Exchanges With Democrats
item.description: The remarks, at a hearing ostensibly about the coming budget for law enforcement agencies, veered from sedate exchanges about operational matters to ugly personal confrontations.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kash Patel, the director of the F.B.I., sparred with Democratic lawmakers on Tuesday on Capitol Hill.

item.title: Heated Exchange Between F.B.I. Director and Senator During Hearing
item.description: Senator Chris Van Hollen, Democrat of Maryland, questioned the director of the F.B.I., Kash Patel, about reports of excessive drinking and ordering polygraph tests for members of his team in a search for sources of leaks about him.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kash Patel, the director of the F.B.I., sparred with Democratic lawmakers on Tuesday on Capitol Hill.

item.title: Jason Collins, First Active N.B.A. Player to Come Out as Gay, Dies at 47
item.description: His achievements on the court were eclipsed by an essay he wrote in Sports Illustrated in 2013 in which he declared: “I’m a 34-year-old N.B.A. center. I’m Black and I’m gay.”
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb
item.category: Same-Sex Marriage, Civil Unions and Domestic Partnerships

media:content.media:description: Jason Collins in 2014, the year after he announced he was gay in a front-page essay in Sports Illustrated magazine.

item.title: Kids Review “American Girls” by Harry Styles
item.description: We weren’t sure what to make of Harry Styles’s “American Girls,” so we asked the next generation of music critics. The kids break it down with our critic Jon Caramanica on this special edition of the “Popcast” Song of the Week.
item.dc:creator: Jon Caramanica, Arjun Srivatsa and Joe Coscarelli

item.title: West Virginia Primary 2026: Live Election Results
item.description: Get live results and maps from the 2026 West Virginia primary elections.

item.title: Nebraska Secretary of State Primary 2026: Live Election Results
item.description: Get live results and maps from the 2026 Nebraska primary election.
